inference.title: Inference Brief
inference.summary: A working AI-news reader built for people who want the signal without living in feeds.
inference.imageAlt: Inference Brief issue and article interface
inference.problem: AI news is noisy, repetitive, and easy to over-skim. Inference Brief turns source collection, scoring, summarisation, issue publishing, and reader history into one product flow.
points: Collects and scores stories before they become reader-facing issues.
Packages summaries, bookmarks, account history, and preferences around the reading job.
Built as a real product rather than a static newsletter archive.
inference.note: Best opened as the live product; the preview sets the context before you leave the store.

happening.title: Happening
happening.summary: A venue-event ingestion system that turns inconsistent public pages into checked event data.
happening.imageAlt: Happening event feed, source drawer, and map interface
happening.problem: Venue listings are scattered across pages with different layouts, calendars, markup, and failure modes. Happening treats each source as a configured data contract with extraction, dedupe, validation, and regression tests.
points: Uses source configs so behaviour is explicit and reviewable.
Combines crawling, extraction, dedupe, checks, and scheduled runs.
Backed by a 167-test private reliability suite across the ingestion surface.
happening.note: The preview shows the product surface and the private case study explains the reliability work behind it.

quicksupply.title: QuickSupply
quicksupply.summary: A supply-cover workflow for requests, agencies, teacher offers, compliance, and live status.
quicksupply.problem: School cover coordination can become a spreadsheet-and-message chain. QuickSupply shapes the job around request intake, sequential agency assignment, teacher availability, compliance checks, and status updates.
points: Separates school, agency, and teacher workflows instead of flattening them into one admin view.
Uses live status updates so bookings can move without manual chasing.
Designed around operational clarity: who has the request, what changed, and what happens next.
quicksupply.note: The demo gives a quick pass through the agency workflow: request view, assignment context, and live booking status.

study.title: AI Study Companion
study.summary: A study workflow for turning uploaded notes into flashcards, quizzes, plans, and review sessions.
study.problem: Revision material often starts as a pile of documents and notes. This product shape turns those inputs into structured practice: cards, quizzes, plans, and spaced review.
points: Ingests PDF, DOCX, and text material before generating study assets.
Separates background generation jobs from the learner-facing review flow.
Uses a spaced-repetition loop so the product is about retention, not just content generation.
study.note: This is a private build, so the preview focuses on the workflow and system decisions rather than exposing code.

smart-job.title: Smart Job Market Intelligence
smart-job.summary: A labour-market signal layer for turning listings into salary, skill, remote-work, and volume trends.
smart-job.problem: Job listings are noisy snapshots. This system normalises listings and turns them into repeatable reports and alerts that are easier to compare over time.
points: Normalises listing data so trend analysis has a consistent base.
Packages salary, skill, volume, and remote-work signals into usable outputs.
Frames the product around alerts, reports, and API tiers rather than one-off analysis.
smart-job.note: The preview keeps the emphasis on the decision-support workflow: collect, normalise, compare, alert.

operations.title: Operations Platform Prototype
operations.summary: A property-operations concept for resident requests, audit trails, payments, and AI-assisted triage.
operations.problem: Operational requests get messy when status, ownership, evidence, payment context, and triage notes live in different places. This prototype brings that work into one interface model.
points: Models resident request intake and operations follow-up as a traceable workflow.
Includes audit trail and service-charge visibility concepts.
Explores where AI triage can help without hiding operational accountability.
operations.note: This sits in the store as a product prototype: useful for showing product thinking, not as a public repo.

lakehouse.title: Marketing ML Lakehouse
lakehouse.summary: A local analytics loop that moves raw marketing CSVs through DuckDB, model training, checks, and a dashboard.
lakehouse.problem: Many analytics demos stop at a notebook. This repo packages the full local loop: ingest raw marketing data, model it, check it, and expose it through a dashboard.
points: Uses a bronze, silver, and gold flow to make the data path inspectable.
Includes model training and data-quality checks rather than only charts.
Has repo packaging and validation so reviewers can inspect it as a runnable project.
lakehouse.note: A strong public repo to inspect: end-to-end data flow, model training, tests, and a dashboard in one place.

projectlens.title: ProjectLens
projectlens.summary: A schedule-risk app for turning project updates into slippage, milestone, and forecast outputs.
projectlens.problem: Schedule data can be hard to read until the risk is already obvious. ProjectLens packages upload, cleaning, slippage analysis, milestone pressure, and reporting into a clearer workflow.
points: Turns project schedule inputs into risk and slippage outputs.
Packages analysis into reporting views rather than leaving it as raw tables.
Includes tests and validation so the public repo is more reviewer-friendly.
projectlens.note: The repo is packaged for review with a clear workflow, tests, and public inspection path.

architexa.title: Architexa
architexa.summary: A compact architecture image-generation project built around dataset prep, conditional GAN training, and a small API.
architexa.problem: The project explores image generation for architecture-style outputs, including how the data is prepared, how the model is trained, and how generated outputs can be served.
points: Curates image data before model training.
Uses a conditional GAN workflow for generation.
Exposes model interaction through a small Flask API.
architexa.note: A focused ML build that shows dataset preparation, model training, and a simple serving path.

recommender.title: Dating App Recommendation System
recommender.summary: A recommendation project focused on implicit-feedback ranking, temporal holdouts, and Top-K metrics.
recommender.problem: Recommendation demos often skip the evaluation question. This project uses swipe-style interaction data and temporal splitting to make ranking performance easier to reason about.
points: Builds candidate ranking from implicit interaction data.
Uses temporal holdouts so evaluation is closer to real recommendation timing.
Includes sample data, tests, and a CLI smoke path for public review.
recommender.note: This preview is about evaluation discipline: how the recommender is tested matters as much as the model choice.

ai-evaluator.title: AI Workflow Evaluator
ai-evaluator.summary: A small evidence gate that decides whether AI-generated output can ship, needs review, or should be blocked.
ai-evaluator.problem: AI-generated summaries and recommendations can sound polished while quietly overclaiming. This project checks each output against source evidence, required facts, blocked claims, latency, cost, and review status.
points: Uses deterministic rules, so the sample suite runs locally without API keys.
Produces ship, review, or block decisions with evidence notes.
Includes baseline deltas, dataset/scorer versions, trace evidence, tests, and GitHub Actions validation.
ai-evaluator.note: A compact public repo that shows how I think about AI product reliability: evidence first, criteria visible, review gates explicit, calibration shown.

hr.title: HR Performance Analytics
hr.summary: A Power BI dashboard package for headcount, sales performance, absence, and stakeholder-ready reporting.
hr.problem: Dashboard work is only useful when someone can understand the business question and inspect the handoff material. This package keeps screenshots, PBIX files, data, and commentary together.
points: Covers summary, sales, and absence views.
Includes prepared data and dashboard files for inspection.
Frames the work as a handoff package rather than a single screenshot.
hr.note: The repo is framed as a dashboard handoff package with files, screenshots, and commentary together.

sentence.title: Sentence Similarity Analysis
sentence.summary: A compact NLP notebook showing embeddings, cosine ranking, and the limits of similarity as a signal.
sentence.problem: Similarity search is useful, but similarity is not the same as truth. This notebook is a small, focused demo of embedding comparison and retrieval caveats.
points: Uses transformer embeddings to compare sentence meaning.
Ranks examples with cosine similarity.
Calls out where retrieval-style scoring can mislead.
sentence.note: This is intentionally small: it works as a clear NLP concept demo in the broader portfolio.

archive.title: Newsletter + Scraper Utilities
archive.summary: Earlier automation utilities kept for provenance: newsletter rendering and image collection patterns.
archive.imageAlt: Newsletter and scraper utilities archive visual
archive.problem: Not every project should be presented as current flagship work. These utilities show earlier automation patterns that fed later product and data experiments.
points: Article-to-HTML newsletter rendering helped shape later publishing workflows.
Image collection utilities supported earlier dataset experiments.
Kept in the store as archive context, not as the first thing to inspect.
archive.note: This preview is deliberately honest: archived work can be useful evidence without competing with the strongest builds.
